brand.name: Revamp (Its Revamp)
brand.vertical: B2B/professional services — digital marketing agency (SEO, web design, lead generation)

offer.what: Integrated digital marketing retainer combining SEO-optimized website design, local/national SEO, lead generation, PPC advertising, and social media marketing — packaged as a 'Digital Revenue Engine' rather than a menu of standalone services
offer.coreOutcome: Predictable, consistent leads and revenue growth; clients achieve measurable market leadership and a robust sales pipeline
offer.pricePoint: Custom/undisclosed; web design engagements estimated $5,000–$25,000+; full-service retainers likely $1,500–$5,000/month
offer.commitment: Ongoing retainer (recurring); project-based web design as entry point

audience.icp: Local and regional business owners in competitive service verticals (law firms, HVAC, roofing, plumbing, tree services, professional services) primarily in San Antonio / Texas, seeking revenue-focused digital marketing rather than commodity SEO
painPoints: Invisible on Google — losing leads to better-ranked competitors
Paying for marketing that can't demonstrate ROI
No unified digital strategy — disjointed website, SEO, and ads
Too busy running the business to manage digital marketing themselves
segments: Home-service contractors (HVAC, roofers, plumbers) — high local search volume, seasonal urgency
Legal / professional services (law firms, accountants) — high ticket, reputation-sensitive
Healthcare / med-spa / wellness — local competition, trust-driven
Restaurants and retail — Google Maps & reviews-dependent

salesCycle.length: Days to weeks — initial free audit/assessment → consultation call → proposal → close
salesCycle.trial: Free Google Maps ranking assessment and free SEO audit serve as the entry-point trial offer
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Booked discovery/strategy call; proposal delivered after audit; salesperson or founder closes
salesCycle.topObjection: 'I've been burned by SEO agencies before and didn't see results' — credibility and proof of ROI is the primary barrier

ahaMoment: Client sees their business rank on page 1 of Google (or Google Maps pack) for a high-intent local keyword and receives their first organic lead — typically within 60–90 days of onboarding

retention.recurring: Yes — monthly retainer for ongoing SEO, content, ads management, and reporting
retention.expansion: Upsell from SEO-only to full 'Revenue Engine' (add PPC, social media, content marketing, reputation management); add additional service-area campaigns
retention.churnRisk: Slow ranking progress in first 90 days; client not seeing leads yet; mis-set expectations on SEO timeline; lack of communication/reporting

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — new clients need a structured onboarding sequence explaining the 90-day ramp, what to expect, and how to track progress
Lead-Magnet Nurture — free audit leads likely fall into a cold bucket with no follow-up sequence to convert them to paid clients
Trial/Consult Conversion — no evidence of an automated follow-up sequence post-free-audit to move prospects to a booked call
Value Newsletter — no regular educational/authority content keeping past clients and warm leads engaged
Renewal & Expansion — no structured sequence to present upsell packages at key milestones (e.g., 6-month, 1-year mark)
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — churned clients and old audit leads likely receive no re-engagement outreach
toneVoice: Professional and authoritative with a results-first edge — confident, direct, outcome-obsessed. Speaks in revenue and ROI language rather than marketing jargon. Consultative but not academic. Slight 'big agency energy' for a local firm.
compliance: CAN-SPAM compliant (US-based B2B); no HIPAA or financial compliance triggers; standard unsubscribe and physical address required
